- next_command = 'L 13.59,7.93' (Line to this point)

Given:
- prev_command = 'M 14.47,54.89' (Move to this point)
- current_command = 'L 45.77,29.11' (Line to this point)
- next_command = 'L 36.62,52.57' (Line to this point)

The incoming segment goes from (14.47, 54.89) to (45.77, 29.11)
The outgoing segment goes from (45.77, 29.11) to (36.62, 52.57)

Let me calculate the directions:
- Incoming vector: (45.77 - 14.47, 29.11 - 54.89) = (31.30, -25.78)
- Outgoing vector: (36.62 - 45.77, 52.57 - 29.11) = (-9.15, 23.46)

To find the angle between these vectors, I can use the dot product formula:
- dot product = 31.30 × (-9.15) + (-25.78) × 23.46 = -286.345 - 604.58 = -890.925
- magnitude of incoming = √(31.30² + 25.78²) = √(979.69 + 664.61) = √1644.3 ≈ 40.55
- magnitude of outgoing = √(9.15² + 23.46²) = √(83.72 + 550.37) = √634.09 ≈ 25.18

θ ≈ 150.6° (an obtuse angle)
